lessons.title: Hello! Introduce Yourself
lessons.system_prompt: Teach the student to say: 'My name is [name]', 'I am from [country]', 'I work as [job]', 'Nice to meet you'. Use simple, friendly language. American variant: casual, warm tone. Keep all sentences at A1 level — short, simple, clear.

steps.teacher_prompt: Hi there! I'm so happy to meet you today. Can you tell me your name? Just say: 'My name is...' and then your name!
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their name using 'My name is [name]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Great! Now let's learn how to say where you're from. You say: 'I am from [country]'. For example, I might say 'I am from the United States.' Now, where are you from?
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their country using 'I am from [country]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Wonderful! Now let's talk about your job. You say: 'I work as a [job]'. For example: 'I work as a teacher.' What do you do for work?
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their job using 'I work as a [job]'

steps.teacher_prompt: You're doing amazing! Now let's put it all together. Introduce yourself completely: your name, where you're from, and your job. Go ahead!
steps.expected_student_response: Student gives a full self-introduction combining all three phrases

steps.teacher_prompt: Excellent work today! You learned how to introduce yourself in English. You can now say your name, where you're from, and your job. Keep practicing and you'll get even better!

lessons.title: Hello! Introduce Yourself
lessons.system_prompt: Teach the student to say: 'My name is [name]', 'I am from [country]', 'I work as [job]', 'Lovely to meet you'. Use simple, polite language. British variant: slightly formal, courteous tone. Keep all sentences at A1 level — short, simple, clear.

steps.teacher_prompt: Hello! Lovely to meet you today. Could you tell me your name, please? Try saying: 'My name is...' and then your name.
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their name using 'My name is [name]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Splendid! Now, let's learn how to say where you come from. You say: 'I am from [country]'. For instance, one might say 'I am from the United Kingdom.' Where are you from?
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their country using 'I am from [country]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Brilliant! Now let's talk about your profession. You say: 'I work as a [job]'. For example: 'I work as an engineer.' What is your occupation?
steps.expected_student_response: Student says their job using 'I work as a [job]'

steps.teacher_prompt: Well done! Now, could you introduce yourself fully? Tell me your name, where you're from, and what you do. Take your time.
Marvellous work today! You've learned how to introduce yourself in English. You can now say your name, where you're from, and your job. Carry on practising and you'll do wonderfully!
